concepts.name: AI & LLM Foundations
concepts.description: Foundational landscape of AI in 2026: AI taxonomy (ML/DL/GenAI), how LLMs work, and the core vocabulary every AI builder needs.

concepts.name: Framing AI Problems & AI Product Thinking
concepts.description: Turning a vague 'AI chatbot' idea into a well-scoped problem; the three pillars of an AI product and the four core framing questions.

concepts.name: From Chatbot to Agentic Agents
concepts.description: The Bot -> Chatbot -> Agent spectrum and the ReAct (Reasoning + Acting) loop that turns a model into an autonomous agent.

concepts.name: Prompt Engineering Fundamentals
concepts.description: Prompt as the interface between intent and model capability: the four components of a good prompt and core prompting techniques.

concepts.name: Tool Calling & Context Engineering
concepts.description: Giving agents reliable access to external tools/functions and engineering the context window so agents act dependably.

concepts.name: AI Product Kickoff & Handling Uncertainty
concepts.description: Project kickoff sprint: why a working agent still fails to get adopted; designing for AI's inherent uncertainty vs traditional software.

concepts.name: AI Product Prototyping & Demo
concepts.description: Mini-hackathon: rapidly building, integrating and demoing an end-to-end AI product prototype (MVP).

concepts.name: Data Foundations & Data Strategy
concepts.description: Data strategy for AI products: garbage-in/garbage-out, data quality, and how data decisions drive model output quality.

concepts.name: Embeddings & Vector Stores
concepts.description: Representing text as dense vectors, the embedding space, and storing/searching embeddings in a vector store for semantic retrieval.

concepts.name: RAG Pipeline (Indexing & Retrieval)
concepts.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: the offline indexing/ingestion pipeline and the runtime retrieval pipeline to ground LLM answers.

concepts.name: Multi-Agent Systems & System Connectivity
concepts.description: Moving from single-agent to multi-agent patterns and connecting agents to systems via MCP and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocols.

concepts.name: Data Pipeline & Data Observability
concepts.description: Building robust data pipelines and observing data freshness/quality so agents are not poisoned by stale or dirty input data.

concepts.name: Guardrails & AI Safety
concepts.description: Common threat classes against agents and how to add input/output guardrails to make agentic systems safe and controllable.

concepts.name: Cloud Infrastructure & Deployment
concepts.description: Cloud infrastructure components and deploying an agent to the cloud: providers, compute, and a basic deployment decision framework.

concepts.name: Monitoring, Logging & Observability
concepts.description: Monitoring vs observability, logging/tracing for AI systems, and applying control-theory thinking to keep production systems healthy.

concepts.name: AI Evaluation & Benchmarking
concepts.description: Why traditional ML metrics fall short for generative AI; the evaluation paradigm shift and benchmarking methods for LLM systems.

concepts.name: Real-World Deployment, Operating Cost & Specialization Orientation
concepts.description: Enterprise deployment realities, anatomy of operating cost, a 15-day capstone review, and orientation toward the three specialization tracks.

concepts.name: Specialization Track Selection & Advanced Foundations
concepts.description: Branch point: overview of the three tracks (AI Product Strategy, Cloud Infrastructure for AI, Advanced Agent Architectures) and selecting a track.

concepts.name: [T1] PRD & Product-Market Fit
concepts.description: Writing a PRD and reaching product-market fit using the Lean Product Pyramid: MVP boundaries, hypotheses, and a PMF scorecard.

concepts.name: [T1] AI Financial Modeling & ROI
concepts.description: Burn rate, runway, and modeling the ROI of AI initiatives; why ~90% of AI products fail commercially and how to quantify value.

concepts.name: [T1] Stakeholder Management & Investor Pitch
concepts.description: Mapping the stakeholder ecosystem, securing investor buy-in, and pitching to cross the commercialization 'valley of death'.

concepts.name: [T1] Product Roadmap & Execution
concepts.description: Output vs outcome thinking and the frameworks to prioritize, sequence, and execute an AI product roadmap after funding.

concepts.name: [T1] AI Governance & Risk
concepts.description: Startup AI governance: the 3 R's of risk, shifting mindset from 'winning' to 'not dying', and building lightweight policy.

concepts.name: [T1] AI Compliance
concepts.description: Regulatory and legal compliance for AI products, learning from real enforcement cases and operationalizing compliance controls.

concepts.name: [T1] Change Management & AI Adoption
concepts.description: Driving organizational AI adoption: change management, proven adoption tactics, and distinguishing 'wow' from durable adoption.

concepts.name: [T1] Responsible AI
concepts.description: Responsible AI in practice: mapping failure modes, asking who is affected, and moving from values to concrete testing.

concepts.name: [T2] AI Data Engineering Foundations
concepts.description: Track-2 bridge from cloud infrastructure into data engineering: data storage paradigms, ingestion, and platform foundations for AI workloads. (Inferred: Day 17 Track 2 material not present in source repository.)

concepts.name: [T2] Data Lakehouse Architecture
concepts.description: Evolution of data platforms and the lakehouse architecture (storage + table format + compute) for AI/analytics workloads.

concepts.name: [T2] Vector Store & Feature Store
concepts.description: Production embedding/vector stores and feature stores: embedding model selection, indexing, and serving features to AI systems.

concepts.name: [T2] Model Serving & Inference Optimization
concepts.description: Latency taxonomy (TTFT/TPOT/Goodput), serving infrastructure, and inference optimization techniques such as quantization.

concepts.name: [T2] CI/CD for AI Systems
concepts.description: Continuous integration/delivery for ML/AI with experiment tracking and a model registry (e.g. MLflow) across the pipeline.

concepts.name: [T2] LLMOps & Prompt Versioning
concepts.description: LLMOps vs traditional MLOps, prompt versioning, and operating LLM applications reliably in production.

concepts.name: [T2] Monitoring & Observability Stack
concepts.description: Building a full observability stack for AI systems: traces, metrics, and logs tailored to LLM-specific failure modes.

concepts.name: [T2] Data Governance & Security
concepts.description: Data governance for AI: classification, lineage, least-privilege access (RBAC/ABAC, IAM), and securing sensitive data.

concepts.name: [T3] Agent Memory Systems
concepts.description: Why agents are stateless by default and how to engineer memory: the context-engineering framework and the 7 context layers.

concepts.name: [T3] Production RAG
concepts.description: Why basic RAG fails in production: online/offline pipeline failure points and error-tree analysis to debug each stage.

concepts.name: [T3] GraphRAG & Knowledge Graphs
concepts.description: When flat/vector RAG fails on relational questions: building knowledge graphs and GraphRAG for multi-hop retrieval.

concepts.name: [T3] Multi-Agent Systems (Advanced)
concepts.description: Advanced multi-agent taxonomy and orchestration: when to use multiple agents and the coordination patterns that work in production.

concepts.name: [T3] Fine-tuning LLMs (LoRA/QLoRA)
concepts.description: When to fine-tune vs prompt vs RAG; parameter-efficient fine-tuning with LoRA/QLoRA and API vs self-hosted trade-offs.

concepts.name: [T3] DPO, ORPO & Alignment
concepts.description: Beyond SFT: preference learning and alignment (RLHF, DPO, ORPO) to shape model behavior, not just output format.

concepts.name: [T3] LangGraph & Agentic Orchestration
concepts.description: When linear chains are not enough: orchestrating stateful, branching, cyclic agent workflows with LangGraph.

concepts.name: [T3] RAGAS, LLM-as-Judge & Guardrails
concepts.description: Advanced evaluation for generative systems: RAGAS metrics, LLM-as-Judge, and production guardrails for RAG/agents.

concepts.name: [T1] AI Product Strategy & Market Analysis
concepts.description: Từ insight khởi nguồn đến định nghĩa sản phẩm: customer segment, need map, strategy, moat và tính toán market size.

concepts.name: [T2] Cloud Infrastructure for AI
concepts.description: Lựa chọn cloud provider, GPU compute environment, tối ưu chi phí, Terraform IaC, K8s và serving stack vLLM/SGLang.

concepts.name: [T3] Advanced Agent Architectures
concepts.description: Điểm yếu của ReAct, mô hình Reflexion tự phản tỉnh, thuật toán LATS và Voyager.
